title: Slack outbox drain — plain-language rewrite enforced
summary: The .claude/hooks/outbox-to-slack.sh prompt now instructs the claude -p drain subprocess to rewrite outbox entry title + summary fields into plain language before posting to mcp__claude_ai_Slack__slack_send_message. Target 6th-8th grade reading level. Rules: short sentences (15-20 words), active voice, common words over jargon (WCAG/aria-label/SVG-node terminology mapped to readable equivalents), spelled-out acronyms, no code snippets or file paths or A11Y-NNN IDs in the rendered Slack message, lead with what changed for the reader not the criterion ID, bullets for 3+ items, explain why when non-obvious, no double negatives, no emojis other than urgency siren. Original technical text preserved in archived JSON for traceability per the existing drain protocol. README at /agent-outbox/README.md updated with the rewrite rules so agents know summaries get auto-translated and can continue writing in their technical voice.
